---
title: "Euclid × SPT: the simulation landscape"
occasion: SPT Secondaries and Cross-Correlations Call
description: The simulation landscape for Euclid × CMB lensing / secondaries cross-correlations
draft: true
author:
  name: Cail Daley & Euclid CMBX team
date: June 1, 2026
date-format: long

output-file: index.html

format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fig-align: center
    include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
    footer: Cail Daley | Euclid × SPT simulations | SPT Secondaries & Cross-Correlations | June 1, 2026
    history: false
    hash: true
    pdf-separate-fragments: true
    pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    center-title-slide: false

html-math-method:
  method: mathjax
  url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## What simulations have prior cross-correlation analyses used?

Simulations do two jobs: <br>
build or validate the **covariance**, and check for **foreground / pipeline bias**.

::: {.r-stack style="min-height:780px;align-content:center"}

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out style="width:1550px"}
**DES Y3 × SPT + Planck**  ·  *Omori, Chang 2023*  ·  $\delta_g,\, \gamma \times \kappa$

- **signal covariance:** halo-model for the DES 3×2pt block, lognormal for the κ cross-terms
- **noise covariance:** 300 CMB-lensing reconstruction sims (recon − input)
- **foregrounds:** tSZ-nulled lensing map; checked with κ × CIB and cluster stacks
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out style="width:1550px"}
**ACT-DR4 × DES shear**  ·  *Shaikh 2024*  ·  $\gamma \times \kappa$

- **signal covariance:** 511 FLASK lognormal mocks
- **noise covariance:** ACT reconstruction sims, added onto the FLASK signal
- **foregrounds:** tSZ-deprojected map; tSZ-free vs. with-tSZ comparison
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out style="width:1550px"}
**SPT-3G D1 × DES Y3**  ·  *Ouellette+, in prep*  ·  $\gamma \times \kappa$

- **covariance:** Gaussian term from NaMaster (iNKA) evaluated on the *measured* spectra (carries the real noise), plus analytic SSC + cNG
- **foregrounds:** polarization-only reconstruction; temperature-estimator bias quantified in Agora
:::

:::


## The Euclid CMBX simulation ecosystem {.smaller}

A non-exhaustive list. Some pieces live on the CMB / large-scale-structure side, some on the Euclid side:

| Mock | CMB / large-scale side | Euclid side |
|---|---|---|
| **Agora** | coherent multi-probe **foreground** sky (CMB-side N-body) | "Euclidise" via GLASS (needs density shells + halo catalogs) |
| **Flagship2** | — *(ΛCDM N-body, no κ_CMB)* | official Euclid **galaxy + WL** mock; gold-standard realism, the **bias & *n(z)* reference** |
| **DEMNUni** | correlated **κ_CMB** + galaxies + WL (ΛCDM, massive-ν, *f(R)*) | bias & *n(z)* from **Flagship2** → **numerical covariances** |
| **Contaminated GLASS** | — | inject **Euclid systematics**, watch the cross-spectrum |
| **Per-experiment recon suites** | each experiment's **κ-reconstruction sims** | **per-experiment noise** |


## Euclid-like Agora for systematics characterization

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:52%"}

- I've been chatting with **Marco Gatti** about building **Euclid-like products from Agora** on NERSC

- To do this we'd just need Agora's **density shells + halo catalogs**. Do those already exist on
  NERSC, or do we download them from **Globus**?

- We could even **inject Euclid-like systematics** into the Euclid side, for a suite with systematics
  on **both the CMB and Euclid sides**

:::
::: {.column style="width:48%"}

![](images/agora_maps.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}

::: {style="font-size:0.6em;text-align:center;margin-top:-15px"}
Omori (2024)
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## How do we account for SPT × ACT × Planck correlations?

- A joint data vector puts **SPT-κ × Euclid** and **ACT-κ × Euclid** side by side, so the two
  measurements are correlated

- **Qu, Ge et al (2026)** met this for the **auto-spectra**: combining ACT + SPT + Planck κκ, they
  derived the cross-experiment covariance **analytically** — shared lensing signal *and* correlated
  reconstruction noise, verified with sims. It's **small** (ACT–SPT $\lesssim15\%$, Planck–SPT
  $\lesssim10\%$): little sky overlap, and SPT is **polarization-only** while ACT / Planck are
  temperature-dominated

<br>

- **But that argument is about the κ maps.** In cross-correlation both spectra share the **same Euclid
  tracer**, correlated through its **sample variance** — which doesn't care about κ-reconstruction
  overlap or T-vs-pol, and is *not* negligible

- **The open question:** how do we estimate the **SPT × ACT cross-covariance** for a
  shared-Euclid-tracer data vector? (shared signal, independent reconstruction noise)
